spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the feature's general idea but doesn't surface the named spec API symbols directly.

interactive.rationale: Interactive surface exists but is limited — more controls or a richer live readout would help.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Most of the blurb's promises land but a few of the named behaviours aren't shown.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is covered but only a few failure modes are surfaced.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link as required.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex, button overrides keep bg+color paired.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for x25519kyber768-key-encapsulation-for-tls
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: X25519Kyber768Draft00 hybrid TLS NamedGroup (0x6399). Client share = X25519 pub (32) || Kyber768 pub (1184) = 1216 bytes. Server share = X25519 pub (32) || Kyber ciphertext (1088) = 1120 bytes. Combined secret = X25519 secret (32) || Kyber secret (32) = 64 bytes, fed into TLS 1.3 KDF. Security holds if either component is secure. Pre-standards Kyber; expected to be replaced by ML-KEM (NamedGroup 0...

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently shows the happy path. Add a UI affordance that drives the feature into a failure mode the spec explicitly calls out (timeout, denied permission, cross-origin block, malformed input) and surface what the browser actually does.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface
openQuestions.detail: Add more controls / a richer live readout so the user can drive the feature through its full input space, not just a single canned example.

openQuestions.title: Reconcile blurb and page contents
openQuestions.detail: The feature index blurb promises behaviours that the page doesn't yet exercise. Either trim the blurb to match what the page does, or add the missing UI.

summary: Concept needs follow-up work before it really lands. 4 open question(s) queued.
